claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start by gathering some information about your situation.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I'll need to ask you some questions. Child Benefit eligibility is assessed on a per-child basis, so I'll need to understand your situation for each child.

Let me start with the first question:

**Do you have a child (or children) that you're responsible for? If yes, how old is/are the child/children?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello, yes, I'm responsible for my child Alex. He's 8 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information. So you're responsible for Alex, who is 8 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Since Alex is 8 years old (under 16), he would generally qualify as a qualifying child. Now I need to ask you some questions about your own circumstances.

**Are you responsible for Alex? By this I mean:**
- Does Alex live with you, OR
- Do you contribute at least the same amount as Child Benefit to the cost of supporting Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't live with me currently. But I do contribute towards his upkeep - I help out with money for his things regularly.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. You mentioned that you contribute towards Alex's upkeep regularly.

To determine if you meet the responsibility test, I need to understand the amount you contribute:

**Do you contribute at least the same amount as Child Benefit would provide to the cost of supporting Alex?**

For context, Child Benefit is currently £25.60 per week for the eldest or only child (that's about £111 per month). Do your regular contributions meet or exceed this amount?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I contribute more than that actually. I give about £26 a week towards his costs.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good, thank you. Since you're contributing more than the Child Benefit amount (£26 per week), you would meet the responsibility test even though Alex doesn't live with you.

Next question:

**Is Alex receiving any benefits in his own right, such as Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't receive any benefits in his own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Next question:

**Is Alex in any type of apprenticeship?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in an apprenticeship. He's only 8, so he's just in primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Of course, that makes sense. Now I need to ask about your own circumstances:

**Do you normally live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, you meet the UK residency requirement. We've already established that you meet the responsibility test through your financial contributions.

Now I need to ask:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good. Now I need to understand where Alex lives and his living situation:

**Is Alex currently:**
- In hospital?
- In local authority care?
- Living with foster carers?
- Or living in a normal home situation (even though not with you)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He's living in a normal home situation - he lives with his mum.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question:

**Is the local council paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, the council isn't paying anything towards his accommodation or maintenance.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent news! Based on all the information you've provided, **you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's a summary of why you qualify:

✓ **Alex is a qualifying child** - He's 8 years old (under 16), not receiving benefits in his own right, and not in an apprenticeship

✓ **You meet the responsibility test** - Even though Alex doesn't live with you, you contribute £26 per week towards his costs, which exceeds the Child Benefit amount

✓ **You meet the residency requirement** - You normally live in the UK (Manchester)

✓ **No competing claim** - Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

✓ **No local authority involvement** - The council isn't paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance

**Next steps to claim:**
- Apply online or by post using form CH2
- You'll need proof of identity for yourself and Alex
- You'll need Alex's birth certificate
- Be aware: If you or your partner earns over £60,000 per year, the High Income Child Benefit Charge may apply (which means you might have to pay some or all of it back through tax)
- Payments are typically made every 4 weeks

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit or the claiming process?
